sprint_meta.notes: WK124-T2: Guild / Inn / Market (and blacksmith / trading-post) selected-building MENU panels must show HP like the guardhouse does. Root cause (PM-diagnosed): not a data bug (all buildings get hp=max_hp=200 from base.py:79-80); the selected-building menu renderers GuildPanelRenderer (guild_panel.py) and EconomicPanelRenderer (economic_panel.py) simply omit the HP row, while DefensivePanelRenderer (guardhouse) draws it via _render_hp_block. The damage-gated world-space floating bar in ursina_building_ui.py is a SEPARATE element -- left untouched.

r1.task: WK124-T2 (Wave 1): add an unconditional HP row+bar to the guild and economic selected-building menu panels so guild/inn/market/blacksmith/trading-post match the guardhouse menu. Promote a shared render_hp_block helper into building_renderers/__init__.py and reuse it everywhere. Add tests/test_wk124_building_hp_panels.py.

what_i_changed.game/ui/building_renderers/__init__.py: Added shared render_hp_block(panel, surface, building, y) -> int (mirrors render_occupants placement: defined BEFORE the sub-module imports at lines 34-39 so the name is bound when those modules import it -- same proven pattern render_occupants already uses, no circular import). It renders 'HP: {hp}/{max_hp}' in COLOR_WHITE then a full-width HPBar with good=COLOR_GREEN, identical to DefensivePanelRenderer._render_hp_block. Added imports: COLOR_GREEN (to existing COLOR_GOLD/COLOR_RED/COLOR_WHITE line) and HPBar (from game.ui.widgets).
what_i_changed.game/ui/building_renderers/defensive_pan: Refactored DefensivePanelRenderer._render_hp_block to delegate to the shared render_hp_block helper (one-line return) so all four building families render HP identically. Trimmed now-unused imports (COLOR_GREEN, COLOR_RED, HPBar removed; kept COLOR_WHITE for the guardhouse stats text); added 'from game.ui.building_renderers import render_hp_block'. Behavior byte-identical -> WK61 guardhouse regression still green.
what_i_changed.game/ui/building_renderers/guild_panel.p: Imported render_hp_block; call it at the TOP of GuildPanelRenderer.render (before render_occupants). Shows HP unconditionally for warrior/ranger/rogue/wizard guilds.
what_i_changed.game/ui/building_renderers/economic_pane: Imported render_hp_block; call it inside each sub-renderer AFTER the construction-notice early-return -> _render_marketplace, _render_blacksmith, _render_inn (after the 'if not is_constructed: return notice' guard, before render_occupants) and at the top of _render_trading_post (no construction notice). Placed inside the sub-renderers (not at the top of render()) so the existing dispatch-routing test test_ui_building_renderers.py::test_economic_renderer_dispatches_enum_building_types -- which passes a bare SimpleNamespace panel + surface=None and monkeypatches the sub-renderers -- stays green. This matches the plan's 'only after any construction-notice early-returns' guidance.
what_i_changed.tests/test_wk124_building_hp_panels.py: NEW. Mirrors tests/test_wk61_r5_guardhouse_hp_panel.py panel-construction + clipped-region color-assertion pattern. test_guild_panel_shows_hp (WarriorGuild), test_inn_panel_shows_hp (Inn), test_marketplace_panel_shows_hp (Marketplace): construct building, mark is_constructed=True, select on a BuildingPanel(1920,1080), render to a 1920x1080 surface, clip to panel rect, assert COLOR_WHITE (HP text) and COLOR_GREEN (full HP bar) both present.

r1.approach_note: Used the PREFERRED shared-helper refactor (promote render_hp_block into __init__.py + delegate defensive_panel to it) rather than copy-pasting the block, so HP renders identically across all building families and there is one source of truth. First attempted placing the economic HP block at the top of EconomicPanelRenderer.render(), but that broke the pre-existing dispatch test (bare SimpleNamespace panel has no font_normal); moved it inside each sub-renderer after the construction-notice early-return, which both keeps that test green and satisfies the plan's after-construction-notice requirement.

wk68_g0_diagnose.confirmed_root_cause: H3 (hit-rect coordinate space) -- NOT H1 pause-guard, NOT H2 masked exception.
diagnosis: The building panel renders ALL content into a tall scratch sub-surface (panel_surf, scratch_h up to 2400px), then blits only a CLIPPED, scroll-offset slice into the left-column viewport Rect(panel_x, panel_y, panel_width, viewport_h) with src_y=menu_scroll_px (building_panel.py:333-341).

But every interactive rect is stored at its UN-scrolled scratch position: enter_building_button_rect = Rect(panel_x+local.x, panel_y+local.y, ...) with NO menu_scroll_px term (building_panel.py:502-507); same for demolish (:461-466) and research/blacksmith/library/upgrade/build_catalog in the 3 building_renderers modules. menu_scroll_px appeared NOWHERE in rect computation.

Consequence: when panel content overflows the left-column viewport (hero pinned / 720p / 4-occupant guild), the bottom buttons (Enter renders last/lowest) fall BELOW the clip and are unclickable; scrolling does not help because the stored rect stays frozen at its scratch y while the drawn button moves with src_y.

Evidence lines: building_panel.py:502-507 (enter rect, no scroll term), :461-466 (demolish), :333-341 (clipped scroll-blit src_y=menu_scroll_px), :136-140 (outer bounds-gate). Same pattern in building_renderers/{economic,special,castle}_panel.py. Close (X) at top is unaffected (top-pinned).
ranked_hypotheses_ruled_out: H1 (input_handler.py:421-427 pause-guard) -- left in place; the click reaches building_panel.handle_click fine when the panel is visible. NOT the cause (separate latent issue; Agent 11 owns the paused-click regression test per PM brief).
H2 (try/except at input_handler.py:445) -- no exception fires in the click ladder; the click is simply rejected by the frozen-rect/viewport mismatch. NOT the cause.

diagnosis.mechanism: Headless repro (hero SELECTED + hero PINNED, 1920x1080): clicking the 14x14 header X routes to HUD.handle_click L1144-1148 -> toggles _watch_card_expanded; mouse.py action is a no-op (no deselect). The toggle itself is clean - NOTHING gets deselected. The REAL mechanism is in hud_left_layout.layout_left_column_segments main_open and watch_open branch: the watch segment was sized by the SAVED FRACTION (_left_split_fracs['watch']) regardless of expand/collapse. Default 0.45, and the ui_panels sidebar_split scenario the Sovereign was testing uses 0.68. So even COLLAPSED, the watch card reserved 45-68 percent of the column (rendered as an empty dark box). Expanding merely FILLED that pre-reserved segment -> reads as the card maximizing over the (already-squeezed) main menu.
diagnosis.field_deltas_default_fracs: collapsed->expanded with default 0.55/0.45: main rect (0,48,224,469) ~unchanged; watch (0,517,224,383)->(0,517,224,383). Rects do NOT change on toggle; the 383px watch box is empty when collapsed (only an 18px header drawn).
diagnosis.field_deltas_scenario_fracs: with scenario 0.32/0.68 (what Sovereign saw, watch expanded): main=(0,48,224,273) - squeezed to a tiny strip; watch=(0,321,224,579) - dominates 2/3 of the column. THIS is the reported 'maximizes over the whole sidebar / gets rid of other menus'.
diagnosis.nothing_deselected: selected_hero stayed True, selected_building stayed False across all clicks. handle_click returned 'watch_card_chevron_toggle' each time. Chevron rect re-derived each frame; second click reliably collapses back.

game/ui/hud_left_layout.py: rewrote the main_open and watch_open split (~L86-120) to be CONTENT-bounded instead of fraction-driven. watch_want = WATCH_CARD_HEADER_H when collapsed else _desired_watch_card_expanded_h() (already includes WATCH_CARD_CHAT_H when _chat_visible, so WK115 BUG3 chat-grow is preserved). main_natural = _left_main_natural_h(gs) (hero panel content; building panel falls back to fraction; 0 -> first-frame fraction fallback). watch_h = min(watch_want, available-main_floor); main_h = min(main_natural, available-watch_h); HERO_LEFT_MIN_H clamp kept for genuinely short columns. Net: collapsed watch = 18px header peek (no giant box); expanded watch = its own content only (never full column); main menu always keeps its content (>= MAIN_MENU_MIN_PRESENT_H); leftover column is empty, not inflated.
